AesCrypto.title: AES Encryption & Decryption
AesCrypto.description: Protect your sensitive data with military-grade AES-256-GCM encryption. All processing happens locally in your browser using the Web Crypto API — your data never leaves your device.
AesCrypto.inputPlaceholder: Enter the text you want to encrypt...
AesCrypto.securityNote: Uses Web Crypto API with AES-256-GCM and PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations). A unique Salt and IV are generated for every encryption, ensuring the same plaintext produces different ciphertext each time.
AesCrypto.cipherPlaceholder: Paste the encrypted Base64 string here...
AesCrypto.decryptedPlaceholder: Decrypted original text will appear here...
AesCrypto.decryptNote: Decryption successful. The original text has been recovered. Make sure to keep your password safe — there is no way to recover data without it.
AesCrypto.encryptError: Encryption failed. Please check your inputs.
AesCrypto.decryptError: Decryption failed. Incorrect password or corrupted data.
AesCrypto.step1Desc: Your password is transformed into a 256-bit encryption key using PBKDF2 with 100,000 iterations and a random salt.
AesCrypto.step2Desc: The plaintext is encrypted using AES-256 in GCM mode with a random 96-bit IV, providing both confidentiality and integrity.
AesCrypto.step3Desc: Salt, IV, and ciphertext are combined into a single Base64 string for easy sharing. Decryption requires the exact same password.

guide.title: How to Use AES Encryption
guide.step1: Choose 'Encrypt' mode and enter the text you want to protect. Type a strong password (the strength meter helps you gauge quality).
guide.step2: Click 'Encrypt Text' to generate the encrypted Base64 output. Copy the result and share it securely.
guide.step3: To decrypt, switch to 'Decrypt' mode, paste the encrypted text, enter the same password, and click 'Decrypt Text'.

f1.title: AES-256-GCM
f1.desc: Military-grade encryption standard used by governments and financial institutions worldwide.

f2.title: PBKDF2 Key Derivation
f2.desc: 100,000 iterations of PBKDF2-SHA256 make brute-force attacks computationally infeasible.

f3.title: Unique Salt & IV
f3.desc: Every encryption generates random Salt and IV, so the same text always produces different output.

f4.title: 100% Browser-Based
f4.desc: Uses the native Web Crypto API. No data is ever sent to any server — everything runs locally.

f6.title: Base64 Output

disclaimer.title: Technical Limitations & Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This tool provides estimated data based on public P2P network DHT (Distributed Hash Table) information. Since most home internet connections (ISPs) use 'Dynamic IPs', the retrieved history might belong to a previous user of this IP address, not necessarily you. This result cannot be used as legal evidence and is for reference purposes only.

PortScanner.noScanDesc: Configure a target and press Start Scan to monitor port availability.

legend.openDesc: The service responded and connection was accepted. This usually means an application is listening on this port.
legend.closedDesc: The host responded but explicitly refused connection. No application is currently listening on this port.
legend.filteredDesc: No response was received within the timeout period. This is often caused by a firewall dropping packets.

limitation.title: Note
limitation.content: This tool uses a server-side scanner to check port availability accurately.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter the target hostname or IP address to scan.
guide.step2: Click 'Start Scan' to check common ports.
guide.step3: Review Open, Closed, and Filtered statuses to diagnose service availability.

ImageConverter.title: Image Format Converter
ImageConverter.clickOrDrag: Click or drag image to upload
ImageConverter.introDesc: Upload an image and convert it to PNG, JPG, or WebP format. All processing happens locally in your browser—your images never leave your device.
ImageConverter.description: Batch convert images between PNG, JPG, and WebP formats. Processed locally in your browser.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Drag & drop or click to upload the image you want to convert.
guide.step2: Click the desired format button: PNG, JPG, or WebP.
guide.step3: Download the converted image. All processing happens locally in your browser.

ImageConverter.qualityHint: Applies to JPG and WebP only. PNG is lossless.
ImageConverter.conversionError: Conversion failed. Please try another image.

SqlConverter.title: SQL to JSON/CSV Converter
SqlConverter.description: Transform SQL INSERT statements into JSON or CSV data formats locally.
SqlConverter.errorInvalidFormat: Could not parse format. Please check your input.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter SQL INSERT statements or tab-separated table data.
guide.step2: Choose the output format (JSON, CSV).
guide.step3: Review the converted result and copy it for use.

UuidGenerator.title: UUID Generator
UuidGenerator.description: Generate UUID v4 and v7 with bulk generation and clipboard support.
UuidGenerator.v7Desc: UUID v7: Timestamp-based + random (sortable by time)

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Select UUID v4 or v7 version.
guide.step2: Enter the number of UUIDs to generate and click 'Generate'.
guide.step3: Copy individual UUIDs or use 'Copy All' to copy the entire list.

PasswordGenerator.title: Password Generator
PasswordGenerator.description: Generate secure random passwords with customizable length, characters, and strength meter.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Set the password length using the slider (8-128 characters).
guide.step2: Choose which character types to include: uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols.
guide.step3: Click 'Generate Password' and use the copy button to save to clipboard.

UnixTimestamp.title: Unix Timestamp Converter
UnixTimestamp.description: Two-way conversion between Unix timestamps and human-readable dates.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: The current Unix timestamp is displayed in real-time.
guide.step2: Enter a timestamp to convert it to a human-readable date.
guide.step3: Enter a date/time to convert it to a Unix timestamp.

MetaTagGenerator.title: Meta Tag Generator
MetaTagGenerator.description: Generate SEO-optimized HTML meta tags, Open Graph, and Twitter Card code automatically.
MetaTagGenerator.pageDescriptionPlaceholder: A brief description of your page for search engines
MetaTagGenerator.author: Author
MetaTagGenerator.emptyPreview: Fill in the fields above to generate meta tags.
MetaTagGenerator.previewSiteDesc: Your page description will appear here.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Fill in basic meta information like title, description, and keywords.
guide.step2: Add Open Graph tags for Facebook and social media sharing.
guide.step3: Configure Twitter Card tags for Twitter sharing previews.
guide.step4: Copy the generated HTML code and paste it into your page's head section.

RobotsTxtGenerator.title: Robots.txt Generator
RobotsTxtGenerator.description: Create robots.txt files for search engine crawlers with preset templates.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Select a preset template or create custom rules for search engine crawlers.
guide.step2: Add Allow/Disallow paths to control which pages can be crawled.
guide.step3: Optionally add your sitemap URL and crawl delay settings.
guide.step4: Copy or download the generated robots.txt and place it in your site root.

SitemapGenerator.title: Sitemap Generator
SitemapGenerator.description: Add URLs and generate XML sitemap files to improve search engine indexing.
SitemapGenerator.emptyPreview: Add valid URLs above to generate the sitemap XML.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Add your website URLs with the 'Add URL' button.
guide.step2: Set last modified date, change frequency, and priority for each URL.
guide.step3: Copy or download the generated XML sitemap file.
guide.step4: Upload sitemap.xml to your website root and submit it to search engines.

ColorConverter.title: Color Converter
ColorConverter.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, and HSL formats with live preview and related colors.
ColorConverter.invalid: Invalid color format. Use HEX, RGB, or HSL.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter a color in HEX (#FF5733), RGB (rgb(255,87,51)), or HSL (hsl(11,100%,60%)) format.
guide.step2: Use the color picker or RGB sliders to adjust the color visually.
guide.step3: Copy any converted format and view complementary and analogous colors below.

SubnetCalculator.title: IP Subnet Calculator
SubnetCalculator.description: Calculate network address, broadcast, subnet mask, and usable host range from IP/CIDR.
SubnetCalculator.invalidIp: Invalid IP address. Please enter a valid IPv4 address.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter an IPv4 address (e.g., 192.168.1.0) or use CIDR notation (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24).
guide.step2: Adjust the CIDR prefix with the slider or input field.
guide.step3: View calculated subnet details and copy any value to clipboard.

CssMinifier.title: CSS Minifier / Beautifier
CssMinifier.description: Minify or beautify CSS code with size comparison and instant copy.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Paste your CSS code into the input panel on the left.
guide.step2: Click Minify to compress or Beautify to format the CSS.
guide.step3: View the size comparison and copy the result from the output panel.

JsonLdGenerator.title: JSON-LD Schema Generator
JsonLdGenerator.description: Generate structured data (JSON-LD) for SEO easily.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Select a schema type (Article, Product, FAQ, etc.) from the buttons above.
guide.step2: Fill in the fields. The JSON-LD preview updates in real-time.
guide.step3: Copy the generated script tag and paste it into your HTML.

EnvEditor.title: .env File Editor
EnvEditor.description: Edit environment variable files (.env) and compare differences between two files.
EnvEditor.leftPlaceholder:
Paste .env.example contents here...
e.g.: DATABASE_URL=postgres://...
API_KEY=your-key-here

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Paste your .env.example file on the left and .env.local on the right.
guide.step2: Click 'Compare' to see visual differences between the two files.
guide.step3: In edit mode, add, modify, or remove variables and export as .env file.

TomlConverter.title: TOML/INI Converter
TomlConverter.description: Convert between TOML, INI, and JSON formats.
TomlConverter.inputPlaceholder:
Paste TOML, INI, or JSON code here...

Example:
[database]
host = "localhost"
port = 5432
TomlConverter.parseError: Could not parse input. Please check the format.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Paste TOML, INI, or JSON code in the input panel. The format is auto-detected.
guide.step2: Select the desired output format (TOML, INI, JSON) and click 'Convert'.
guide.step3: Copy the result or use 'Swap' to reverse the conversion.

HttpHeaderAnalyzer.title: HTTP Header Analyzer
HttpHeaderAnalyzer.description: Analyze HTTP response headers from any URL and score security header configuration.
HttpHeaderAnalyzer.errorFetch: Failed to fetch headers. Please check the URL.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter the URL of the website you want to analyze. https:// is added automatically.
guide.step2: Click 'Analyze' to fetch the HTTP response headers from the URL.
guide.step3: Review security header status and evaluate security level through the score and grade.

WebhookTester.title: API Tester
WebhookTester.description: Send REST API requests directly from your browser and analyze responses with an HTTP client tool.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Select an HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.) and enter the request URL.
guide.step2: Add headers and request body (JSON) if needed.
guide.step3: Click 'Send' to view the response status, headers, and body.

GitDiffViewer.title: Git Diff Viewer
GitDiffViewer.description: Paste git diff or unified diff output to visualize changes with syntax highlighting.
GitDiffViewer.placeholder:
Paste git diff or unified diff output here...

Example:
diff --git a/file.txt b/file.txt
--- a/file.txt
+++ b/file.txt
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
 unchanged line
-removed line
+added line
 unchanged line
GitDiffViewer.noValidDiff: No valid diff format found. Please paste git diff output.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Run 'git diff' in your terminal and copy the output.
guide.step2: Paste the diff output in the textarea above. It will be parsed automatically.
guide.step3: Review added/removed lines per file. You can copy individual file diffs.

ExifViewer.title: Image EXIF Viewer & Remover
ExifViewer.description: View and remove EXIF metadata (camera, GPS, date) from images for privacy protection.
ExifViewer.dropTitle: Click or drag image to upload
ExifViewer.dropDesc: Supports JPG, PNG, HEIC, WebP, TIFF
ExifViewer.noExifData: No detailed EXIF data found in this image.
ExifViewer.cleanedNote: Metadata has been stripped. The downloaded image contains no EXIF data.
ExifViewer.privacyDesc: Photos may contain hidden metadata like GPS coordinates, camera info, and timestamps. Remove EXIF data before sharing online to protect your privacy.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Upload an image (JPG, PNG, HEIC) by clicking or drag & drop.
guide.step2: View the extracted EXIF metadata including camera, GPS, and date information.
guide.step3: Click 'Remove EXIF Data' to strip all metadata, then download the clean image.

ImageResizer.title: Image Resizer
ImageResizer.description: Resize images to custom or preset dimensions for social media platforms.
ImageResizer.dropTitle: Click or drag image to upload
ImageResizer.dropDesc: Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and more
ImageResizer.previewPlaceholder: Resized preview will appear here after processing.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Upload an image by clicking or drag & drop.
guide.step2: Select a preset size or enter custom width and height dimensions.
guide.step3: Choose output format and quality, then click 'Resize Image' and download.

SvgOptimizer.title: SVG Optimizer
SvgOptimizer.description: Optimize and minify SVG files by removing unnecessary data and reducing file size.
SvgOptimizer.inputPlaceholder: Paste your SVG code here or upload a .svg file...

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Paste SVG code or upload a .svg file into the input area.
guide.step2: Toggle optimization options like removing comments, metadata, or minifying.
guide.step3: View the before/after size comparison, preview the result, and copy or download.

ScreenshotBeautifier.title: Screenshot Beautifier
ScreenshotBeautifier.description: Add device frames, backgrounds, and shadows to your screenshots for beautiful exports.
ScreenshotBeautifier.dropTitle: Click or drag screenshot to upload
ScreenshotBeautifier.dropDesc: PNG, JPG, WebP supported. You can also paste from clipboard.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Upload or paste a screenshot image.
guide.step2: Choose a device frame, background style, padding, and border radius.
guide.step3: Preview the result and export as a PNG image.

TextToSpeech.title: Text to Speech (TTS)
TextToSpeech.description: Convert text to natural speech with voice selection, speed, and pitch controls.
TextToSpeech.placeholder: Enter text to convert to speech...
TextToSpeech.notSupported: Speech synthesis is not supported in your browser. Please try Chrome, Edge, or Safari.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Type or paste text into the input area.
guide.step2: Select a voice, adjust speed and pitch to your preference.
guide.step3: Click Play to listen. The current word will be highlighted as it is spoken.

TextToSpeech.recordNote: Recording captures tab audio: you'll be asked to share this tab with audio enabled, then click Play.
TextToSpeech.recordNoAudio: No audio track was shared. Enable "Share tab audio".

WhoisLookup.title: Domain / DNS Lookup
WhoisLookup.description: Look up DNS records (A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, SOA) for any domain name.
WhoisLookup.lookupError: Failed to look up DNS records. Please check the domain and try again.
WhoisLookup.noRecords: No DNS records found for this domain.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Enter a domain name (e.g. example.com) in the input field.
guide.step2: Click Lookup to query DNS records for the domain.
guide.step3: Browse the categorized results and copy individual records as needed.

ImageCropper.title: Image Cropper
ImageCropper.description: Crop, rotate, and flip images with aspect ratio presets.

guide.title: How to Use Image Cropper
guide.step1: Upload an image by clicking the upload area or dragging and dropping a file (PNG, JPG, WebP).
guide.step2: Draw a crop region by clicking and dragging on the image. Choose an aspect ratio preset (1:1, 4:3, 16:9, etc.) or use free mode. Use the transform buttons to rotate or flip.
guide.step3: Click 'Download Cropped' to save the cropped image as a PNG file. All processing happens locally in your browser.

f1.title: Aspect Ratio Presets
f1.desc: 6 built-in presets: Free, 1:1, 4:3, 16:9, 9:16, 3:2 for quick cropping.

f2.title: Rule of Thirds Grid
f2.desc: Overlay grid helps you compose shots following the rule of thirds.

f3.title: Rotate & Flip
f3.desc: Rotate 90° and flip horizontally or vertically with one click.

f4.title: 100% Local Processing
f4.desc: Your images never leave your browser. No server uploads.

ImageWatermark.title: Image Watermark
ImageWatermark.description: Add text watermarks to your images.

guide.title: How to Use Image Watermark
guide.step1: Upload an image by clicking the upload area or dragging and dropping a file.
guide.step2: Enter your watermark text and adjust font size, opacity, and color. Choose a position (corners, center) or select tile mode for a repeating pattern across the entire image.
guide.step3: Preview the result in real time and click 'Download' to save. The original image is never modified — a new watermarked copy is created.

f1.title: Tiled Watermark
f1.desc: Repeat watermark across the entire image with adjustable rotation angle.

f2.title: 6 Position Options
f2.desc: Place watermark at any corner, center, or use full-tile coverage.

f3.title: Fine-tune Controls
f3.desc: Adjust font size (12-120px), opacity (5-100%), and any color.

f4.title: Non-destructive
f4.desc: Original image stays untouched. A new watermarked copy is created.

guide.title: How to Use Image Filters
guide.step1: Upload a photo by clicking the upload area or dragging and dropping. Supported formats: PNG, JPG, WebP.
guide.step2: Choose a one-click preset (Vintage, B&W, Warm, Cool, Dramatic, Fade, Vivid) or fine-tune individual sliders for brightness, contrast, saturation, blur, grayscale, sepia, hue rotation, and invert.
guide.step3: Click 'Download' to export the filtered image at full resolution. Click 'Reset' to return all adjustments to their default values.

f1.title: 8 One-Click Presets
f1.desc: Vintage, B&W, Warm, Cool, Dramatic, Fade, Vivid — apply instantly.

f2.title: 8 Adjustment Sliders
f2.desc: Fine-tune brightness, contrast, saturation, blur, grayscale, sepia, hue, invert.

f3.title: Live Preview
f3.desc: See filter changes in real-time as you adjust any slider or select a preset.

f4.title: Full Resolution Export
f4.desc: Download the filtered image at the original resolution, not a thumbnail.

CollageMaker.title: Collage Maker
CollageMaker.description: Create photo collages with custom layouts.

guide.title: How to Use Collage Maker
guide.step1: Select a grid layout (2×1, 1×2, 2×2, 3×1, 1×3, or 3×3) from the settings panel, then click 'Add Image' to upload photos for each slot.
guide.step2: Adjust the gap between images, border radius for rounded corners, background color, and output resolution (up to 3000px). Images are automatically cropped to fit each cell.
guide.step3: Click 'Download Collage' to export as a high-quality PNG. You can remove individual images by hovering and clicking the trash icon.

f1.title: 6 Grid Layouts
f1.desc: Choose from 2×1, 1×2, 2×2, 3×1, 1×3, or 3×3 arrangements.

f2.title: Smart Crop
f2.desc: Images are automatically cropped to fit each cell with cover-fit scaling.

f3.title: Custom Styling
f3.desc: Adjust gap, border radius, background color, and export size up to 3000px.

f4.title: Drag & Drop Upload
f4.desc: Add multiple images at once by dragging files into the tool.

MemeGenerator.title: Meme Generator
MemeGenerator.description: Create memes with custom text overlays.

guide.title: How to Use Meme Generator
guide.step1: Upload a base image by clicking the upload area or dragging and dropping. Any image format works.
guide.step2: Type your top and bottom text in the editor panel. Text is automatically displayed in uppercase Impact font. Adjust font size, text color, stroke color, and stroke width to your liking.
guide.step3: Click 'Download Meme' to save as a full-resolution PNG. The text is rendered with a black stroke outline for maximum readability on any background.

f1.title: Classic Impact Font
f1.desc: Bold uppercase Impact text with configurable stroke for maximum readability.

f2.title: Auto Word Wrap
f2.desc: Long text automatically wraps to multiple lines within the image bounds.

f3.title: Color Customization
f3.desc: Choose any text fill color and stroke color with a color picker.

f4.title: High Quality Export
f4.desc: Download at original image resolution — no quality loss or downscaling.

ColorPicker.title: Image Color Picker
ColorPicker.hint: Click anywhere on the image to pick a color

guide.title: How to Use Color Picker
guide.step1: Upload any image (PNG, JPG, WebP). A pixel-level magnifying loupe will appear in the top-right corner as you move your cursor over the image.
guide.step2: Click on any pixel to pick its color. The color is added to the 'Picked Colors' panel with its HEX, RGB, and HSL values. You can pick as many colors as you need.
guide.step3: Click any color value to copy it to your clipboard. Use 'Clear All' to reset picked colors, or the trash icon to start over with a new image.

f1.title: Pixel-Level Loupe
f1.desc: Magnifying glass shows a zoomed grid of pixels around your cursor position.

f2.title: Multiple Picks
f2.desc: Pick as many colors as you need — all saved in the color history panel.

f3.title: 3 Color Formats
f3.desc: Every pick shows HEX, RGB, and HSL values ready to copy.

f4.title: One-Click Copy
f4.desc: Click any color value to instantly copy it to your clipboard.

MarkdownToPdf.title: Markdown to PDF
MarkdownToPdf.description: Write Markdown and export to PDF.

guide.title: How to Use Markdown to PDF
guide.step1: Write or paste Markdown in the left editor panel. Supports headings, bold, italic, lists, tables, code blocks, blockquotes, images, and links.
guide.step2: Preview the rendered output in real time on the right panel. On mobile, switch between Edit and Preview tabs.
guide.step3: Click 'Export PDF' to open a print dialog for saving as PDF. You can also click 'Copy HTML' to get the rendered HTML markup for use elsewhere.

f1.title: Live Preview
f1.desc: Side-by-side editor and preview update in real time as you type.

f2.title: Rich Syntax Support
f2.desc: Headings, bold, italic, lists, tables, code blocks, blockquotes, images, links.

f3.title: PDF Export
f3.desc: Print-to-PDF with beautiful typography, syntax-highlighted code, and styled tables.

f4.title: Copy as HTML
f4.desc: Export the rendered HTML for embedding in websites or email templates.

CsvEditor.title: CSV Editor
CsvEditor.description: Edit CSV data in a spreadsheet-like table.

guide.title: How to Use CSV Editor
guide.step1: Start editing the default sample data directly, or click 'Import CSV' to load your own CSV file. The first row is treated as column headers.
guide.step2: Click any cell to edit its value. Use 'Column' and 'Row' buttons to add new columns and rows. Hover over row numbers or column headers to reveal delete buttons.
guide.step3: Export your data as CSV or JSON using the toolbar buttons. You can also click 'Copy' to copy the CSV content to your clipboard.

f1.title: Spreadsheet UI
f1.desc: Familiar table interface with click-to-edit cells, similar to Excel or Google Sheets.

f2.title: Import & Export
f2.desc: Load CSV files and export as CSV or JSON with proper escaping and formatting.

f3.title: Dynamic Rows & Columns
f3.desc: Add or remove rows and columns on the fly with toolbar buttons.

f4.title: Clipboard Support
f4.desc: Copy the entire CSV content to clipboard with one click.

guide.title: How to Use Code Formatter
guide.step1: Select the programming language (JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, CSS, HTML, or SQL) from the dropdown menu.
guide.step2: Paste your code in the left panel. Choose indent size (2 or 4 spaces) and toggle semicolons for JS/TS. Click 'Format' to beautify or 'Minify' to compress.
guide.step3: The formatted result appears in the right panel. Click 'Copy' to copy the output to your clipboard. Character counts are shown for both input and output.

f1.title: 6 Languages
f1.desc: Supports JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, CSS, HTML, and SQL formatting.

f2.title: Configurable Style
f2.desc: Choose indent size (2 or 4), toggle semicolons for JS/TS code.

f3.title: Format & Minify
f3.desc: Beautify code for readability or minify it for production in one click.

f4.title: Character Counter
f4.desc: Track input and output character counts to see compression savings.

PomodoroTimer.title: Pomodoro Timer
PomodoroTimer.description: Stay focused with the Pomodoro technique.

guide.title: How to Use Pomodoro Timer
guide.step1: Click the play button to start a 25-minute focus session. The circular progress ring shows your remaining time. The browser tab title updates with a live countdown.
guide.step2: When the timer ends, a sound alert plays and the timer automatically switches to a break (5 min short break, or 15 min long break after every 4 pomodoros).
guide.step3: Open Settings to customize focus duration, short/long break lengths, and the long break interval. Track your total completed pomodoros and focus minutes in the stats panel.

f1.title: Circular Progress
f1.desc: Beautiful animated SVG ring shows remaining time at a glance.

f2.title: Auto Phase Switching
f2.desc: Automatically transitions between focus, short break, and long break.

f3.title: Session Tracking
f3.desc: Track completed pomodoros, total focus minutes, and progress to long break.

f4.title: Sound Alerts
f4.desc: Audio notification plays when each phase ends. Toggle on/off anytime.

ListSorter.title: List Sorter & Deduplicator
ListSorter.description: Organize text lists by sorting alphabetically, removing duplicates, and cleaning whitespace instantly.
ListSorter.placeholder: Enter your list here, one item per line...

guide.title: How to Use List Sorter
guide.step1: Enter or paste your list into the input area. Each item should be on a new line.
guide.step2: Use the action buttons to sort alphabetically, remove duplicate entries, or clean up whitespace.
guide.step3: The processed list is updated in real-time. Use the 'Copy List' button to save the result.

f1.title: Smart Sorting
f1.desc: Sort lists ascending (A-Z) or descending (Z-A) instantly.

f2.title: Deduplication
f2.desc: Identify and remove duplicate lines with a single click.

f3.title: Cleaning Tools
f3.desc: Trim leading/trailing whitespace and remove empty lines automatically.

f4.title: Real-time Stats
f4.desc: Track item counts and see how many duplicates were removed.

JsonToTypes.title: JSON to TypeScript/Zod
JsonToTypes.description: Automatically generate TypeScript interfaces and Zod schemas from your JSON data with custom naming.

guide.title: How to Use JSON to TypeScript
guide.step1: Paste your JSON object into the input panel on the left.
guide.step2: Choose your target format (TypeScript Interface, Type, or Zod Schema) and set a root name.
guide.step3: The generated code appears instantly on the right. Copy it directly into your project.

f1.title: Multiple Formats
f1.desc: Generate TypeScript Interfaces, Type aliases, or Zod validation schemas.

f2.title: Nested Support
f2.desc: Automatically handles complex nested objects and arrays.

f3.title: Smart Naming
f3.desc: Customizable root name and intelligent property naming.

f4.title: Offline-first
f4.desc: All conversion happens locally in your browser for maximum privacy.

CurlToCode.title: CURL to Code Converter

Paste your curl command here...
Example: curl https://api.example.com/data -H "Authorization: Bearer key"

guide.step1: Copy a CURL command from your browser's network tab or terminal.
guide.step2: Paste the command into the input box and select your preferred programming language.
guide.step3: Copy the generated code snippet and paste it into your project.

f4.title: Instant Conversion

HtmlToJsx.title: HTML to JSX Converter
HtmlToJsx.description: Transform standard HTML code into React-compatible JSX. Automatically handles attribute mapping like class to className.

guide.title: How to Use HTML to JSX
guide.step1: Paste your standard HTML code into the input panel.
guide.step2: The tool automatically converts attributes (class → className, for → htmlFor) and styles.
guide.step3: Copy the React-ready JSX and use it in your components.

are properly closed for JSX.

f2.title: Safer Rendering
f2.desc: Prepare untrusted snippets for display by escaping risky characters first.

f3.title: Named & Numeric

MorseConverter.title: Morse Code Converter
MorseConverter.description: Translate text to Morse code and vice versa. Includes audio playback and visual signal support.
MorseConverter.placeholder: Enter text or Morse code (dot . and dash -)...

guide.title: How to Use Morse Converter
guide.step1: Enter English text or Morse code (using . and -) into the input area.
guide.step2: Select the conversion direction and the result will appear instantly.
guide.step3: Listen to the result using the 'Play' button or copy the translated text.

f1.title: Two-way Translation
f1.desc: Seamlessly switch between text-to-morse and morse-to-text.

f2.title: Audio Playback
f2.desc: Hear the morse code sequence with high-quality synthesized beeps.

f3.title: Visual Support
f3.desc: Characters are mapped according to international Morse code standards.

f4.title: Real-time Processing
f4.desc: Translation updates instantly as you type each character.

ui.template:
# [SYSTEM ROLE]
You are {role}. Follow these guidelines to provide an expert answer.

{prompt}

# ROLE
You are {role}.

# TASK
{prompt}

# FORMAT
Respond strictly as {format}.

# CAPACITY & ROLE
Act as {role}.

# INSIGHT
Use relevant background and constraints to inform your answer.

# STATEMENT
{prompt}

# PERSONALITY
Be precise, expert, and direct.

# EXPERIMENT
Provide the answer formatted as {format}.
ui.templateCot:
# ROLE
You are {role}.

# QUESTION
{prompt}

# REASONING
Think step by step, showing each intermediate step before the conclusion.

# OUTPUT
Present the final answer as {format}.
ui.templateFewshot:
# ROLE
You are {role}.

# TASK
{prompt}

# EXAMPLES
Input: [example input]
Output: [example output]

# NOW
Follow the example pattern and respond as {format}.

q1.q: What is the difference between printable and control characters?
q1.a: Codes 32–126 are printable glyphs you can see and type. Codes 0–31 and 127 are control characters (like NUL, ESC, and CR) used for formatting and device control rather than display.

q2.a: The original ASCII standard covers codes 0–127. Codes 128–255 are extended and vary by code page; the values shown here use the Unicode/Latin-1 mapping.

q3.a: No. The table is generated and filtered entirely in your browser, and copying uses your local clipboard. Nothing is uploaded.

guide.title: How to mute a video
guide.step1: Upload your video — MP4, WebM, MOV and more are supported.
guide.step2: Review the preview to confirm it's the right clip.
guide.step3: Click Remove Audio, then preview and download your silent video.

f1.title: Lossless Quality
f1.desc: Video is stream-copied, so the picture stays pixel-for-pixel identical.

f2.title: Lightning Fast
f2.desc: No re-encoding means most videos finish in just a few seconds.

f3.title: 100% Private
f3.desc: Files are processed locally in your browser and never uploaded.

f4.title: Free Forever
f4.desc: No watermarks, no sign-up, and no limits on how many videos you mute.

VideoFrames.title: Video Frame Extractor
VideoFrames.description: Pull image frames out of any video right in your browser using WASM. No upload, fully private.
VideoFrames.inputPlaceholder: Drag & drop a video or click to upload
VideoFrames.selectPrompt: Please select a video file to enable extraction tools

fps.label: Frame Rate (fps)
fps.hint: 1 = one frame per second. Use 0.5 for one frame every 2 seconds.

interval.label: Interval (seconds)
interval.hint: Capture one frame every N seconds. 2 = one frame every 2 seconds.

thumbnail.title: Quick Thumbnail
thumbnail.hint: Grab a single frame at this point in the video.

errors.invalidRate: Enter a valid frame rate greater than 0.
errors.noFrames: No frames were generated. Try a different rate.
errors.zipFailed: Failed to build the ZIP file.

guide.title: How to Use
guide.step1: Drag & drop or click to upload a video file.
guide.step2: Pick a mode — frames per second or a fixed interval — and an image format (PNG or JPG).
guide.step3: Click Extract Frames to preview thumbnails and download all frames as a ZIP, or grab a single thumbnail at a timestamp.

f1.title: Frame Rate Control
f1.desc: Extract at any fps, including fractional rates like one frame every few seconds.

f2.title: PNG or JPG
f2.desc: Choose lossless PNG or smaller JPG output for your extracted frames.

f3.title: Single Thumbnail
f3.desc: Jump to any timestamp and grab one frame instantly.

f4.title: ZIP Download
f4.desc: Bundle every extracted frame into a single ZIP — all processed locally, no uploads.
